landing.introduction: With a range of built-in and extensible Modes, C Code lets you plan, architect, code, debug and boost your productivity like never before.

localAi.title: Set up local AI
localAi.description: Detect your hardware, choose Ollama or LM Studio, and set up a local model.

provider.title: Use an API provider
provider.description: Choose OpenRouter or bring another provider key.

localSetup.heading: Set up local AI
localSetup.subtitle: Answer a few questions and C Code will help set up Ollama or use an existing LM Studio server.

hardware.heading: Hardware and runtime check
hardware.detecting: Detecting your computer and local AI runtimes…
hardware.gpuUnknown: GPU status unknown. We'll stay conservative rather than assuming you have no GPU.

usage.label: How will you use local AI?

preference.label: What should the recommendation optimize for?

privacy.label: Privacy preference
privacy.localPreferred: Prefer local, okay to configure later

provider.label: Local provider
provider.ollamaDescription: Most automated path: C Code can pull the recommended Ollama model with progress.
provider.lmStudioDescription: Use LM Studio if you prefer its app-based model manager and local server.
provider.lmStudioReadyDescription: LM Studio is running with {{models}} available model(s).

lmStudio.ready: Choose one of the models reported by LM Studio, then use it to create a local provider profile.
lmStudio.noModels: LM Studio is reachable, but it reported no usable models. Download or load a chat model in LM Studio, then refresh the check.
lmStudio.serverStopped: LM Studio appears to be installed, but the local server is not running. Start the local server in LM Studio, then refresh the check.
lmStudio.missing: LM Studio is not installed or not reachable. Open the official download page, install LM Studio, download a model, start the local server, then refresh the check.

recommendation.heading: Recommended local model
recommendation.apiDescription: This computer may run local coding models slowly or with limited quality. An API provider is the recommended path for a better first-run experience.
recommendation.manualStillAvailable: You can still choose manual local setup if you want to experiment with Ollama.
recommendation.runtime: Runtime/provider: {{runtime}} from its official local API
recommendation.weakHardwareWarning: Local AI models may be slow or may not work as intended on this hardware.

manual.heading: Manual local AI setup
manual.description: C Code will not run installers silently. Install Ollama or LM Studio yourself, start the local server, then come back and refresh the check.
manual.stepInstall: Download Ollama or LM Studio from the official site.
manual.stepStart: Start Ollama or the LM Studio local server and make sure it is reachable on its default local URL.
manual.stepRetry: Return here, refresh the runtime check, and run the recommendation again.

success.heading: Local AI is ready
success.profile: C Code saved and activated a Local AI (Ollama) provider profile.
success.lmStudioProfile: C Code saved and activated a Local AI (LM Studio) provider profile.

providerSignup.heading: Choose your provider
providerSignup.chooseProvider: C needs an LLM provider to work. Choose one to get started, you can add more later.
providerSignup.useAnotherProviderDescription: Enter an API key and get going.
providerSignup.noApiKeys: Don't want to deal with API keys and separate accounts?

startRouter: We recommend using an LLM Router:
startCustom: Or you can bring your provider API key:
